name: Peppered Seafood Sausage
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Fish Fillets (such as Sole, Flounder, Salmon or Whitefish)
1/2 lb Shrimp and/or Scallops, raw, cleaned
5 Feet Hog Casing (in a continuous length)
recipeInstructions:
Note: If hog casing is unavailable, seven 5-by-10-inch pieces of kitchen parchment may be used instead. See end of recipe for related info.
1. Cut the fish into long strips; shrimp and scallops can remain whole.
2. Put seafood, jalapenos, and cilantro through a coarse blade of a meat grinder.
3. Combine ground seafood with egg whites, lemon juice, cumin, salt, and pepper in a large bowl.
4. Rinse hog casing in cold water by letting water run from the tap directly through it (casing should not have any holes).
5. Gather the casing onto the sausage stuffing funnel attachment on your meat grinder.
6. Put seafood mixture back in the grinder; begin to grind it through.
7. When mixture begins to emerge, tie the end of the casing in a knot for expansion room.
8. Grind mixture out, taking care not to overstuff; sausage should be about 1 inch wide.
9. Continue grinding entire length; you will have approximately 4 feet of sausage.
10. Remove funnel and use fingers to press out last remaining seafood mixture (leave 3 inches for expansion).
11. Tie the end in a knot. Shape evenly, coil into a spiral, and set aside.
- Bring 1 inch of water to boil in a large heavy skillet with lid.
- Reduce heat so that water barely quivers.
- Prick sausage at 3-inch intervals with a sharp knife.
- Place in barely moving water; cover pan and cook for 10 minutes.
- Turn off heat and let rest for 10 minutes before serving.
- If using kitchen parchment, butter paper and place half the mixture on each piece; roll up, twist ends, and tie securely with string. Poach as directed.
